headline: Agent memory and persistent context emerged as the quarter's dominant theme as practitioners moved to fix the amnesia problem in coding agents.
body: A cluster of talks converged on durable state for agents: Victor Savkin framed current coding agents as experts with amnesia lacking persistent context, Ishaan Sehgal argued append-only session logs should anchor agent identity as models commoditize, and Paul Iusztin and Louis-François Bouchard detailed converting notes, documents, and repositories into memory representations. AI context management, scoped skills, LSP, and the PIV Loop led the rotation among AI-assisted development techniques. Production evaluation also advanced, with Meta's Nishant Gupta covering continuous pipelines, drift detection, and human-in-the-loop systems, while OpenGov detailed scaled agent deployment for government using the A2A protocol and long-context handling. A separate wave of new entrants appeared on the math and vision side, including optical flow, RAFT, 360 stereo, Green's functions, and elliptic operators.

cited_items.title: Turn 10,994 Notes Into Memory - Paul Iusztin, Decoding AI & Louis-François Bouchard, Towards AI
Agents in Production: How OpenGov Built and Scaled OG Assist - Gabe De Mesa, OpenGov
A Genius With Amnesia - Victor Savkin, Nx
The Log Is The Agent - Ishaan Sehgal, Omnara
Production Evals For Agentic AI Systems - Nishant Gupta, Meta Superintelligence Labs
